what_it_does: Exports a single React component, Landscape, that renders the wide-short mobile landscape layout for the surface app. It places a wheel/state area on the left, a composed right rail of controls and detail, plus a global scrubber and tether, driven by a single SurfaceState prop.
observations: Single exported functional component named Landscape.
Receives the entire SurfaceState object as prop s.
Renders a root div with class layout layout--landscape.
Left section uses WheelOrState; right aside rail uses stamp('ui://strata/rail').
Rail includes Notice, DecisionsBar, a rail-head with bar-left and bar-right chip groups, CentreChip, a rail-detail panel, and Legend.
rail-detail shows Disclosure when something is selected and the gallery is closed; otherwise it shows SelectHint.
Scrubber and Tether are rendered at the root level outside the rail and center sections.
LensChip is only rendered when s.proj exists.
Comment explicitly targets a 844×390 mobile landscape frame and a ~70%/30% split.
standouts: The file is explicitly framed around a mobile landscape viewport (844×390) and a balanced wheel/rail composition.
DecisionsBar is placed atop the rail with a comment noting landscape has no top header.
The rail-detail avoids an empty panel by always rendering either a Disclosure or a SelectHint.
The rail element is stamped with ui://strata/rail, making the surface addressable.
conventions: React functional component with typed props.
CSS class naming follows BEM-like patterns (layout--landscape, center--left, rail-head, rail-detail, bar-left, bar-right).
Imports type separately from value imports.
concerns: Layout proportions and viewport dimensions are only described in comments, not enforced by code.
A large SurfaceState object is passed through directly, so the component's exact dependencies are implicit.
SelectHint is shown whenever galleryOpen is true even if an item is selected, which may or may not be intended.
No accessibility attributes or semantic headings are visible in the markup.
notes: Landscape is the wide-short mobile layout that wires the wheel, a right-hand control and detail rail, and global scrubber/tether into one composed surface.
questions: What is the exact shape of SurfaceState?
How is the 70%/30% split actually implemented in CSS?
What does galleryOpen represent and why does it suppress the Disclosure?
How is stamp consumed elsewhere in the system?
What is the visual stacking/positioning of Notice, Scrubber, and Tether relative to the layout?
intention_signals: To create a comfortable landscape mobile interface where the wheel remains primary on the left, controls and contextual detail are grouped in a right rail, and the detail area never feels empty.
novelty: The file includes an unusually explicit design comment framing the layout as native to a 844×390 wide-short frame, and it stamps the rail with a ui://strata/rail address.
contribution: Provides the landscape-specific composition of shared components, defining the spatial arrangement, control grouping, and empty-state behavior for wide-short viewports.
summary_for_embedding: A React component that lays out a mobile landscape surface with a left wheel area, a right control-and-detail rail, and global scrubber/tether, switching the rail detail between a disclosure and a selection hint.
intention_for_embedding: To render the wide-short mobile layout where the wheel occupies the left portion and a composed rail presents controls, decisions, and contextual detail without reading as an empty panel.

symbols.does: Renders the mobile landscape layout for the surface app.
symbols.description: Landscape receives a SurfaceState object and composes a wide-short viewport: a left center section containing WheelOrState, a right aside rail holding Notice, DecisionsBar, control chips, a CentreChip, a conditional detail panel that shows Disclosure or SelectHint, a Legend, plus a global Scrubber and Tether. It stamps the rail with a ui://strata/rail data attribute and is intended for a 844×390 landscape frame.
